macro_segments.title: The Relationship Between Nezir Shaving and Entering the Beit Hamikdash: Rabbi Eliezer's Teaching in the Name of Rabbi Yehoshua

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Exceptions in Tumas Hameis and Their Halachic Consequences
Rabbi Meir's Objection: Why Should Lesser Tumot Escape Liability for Biat Mikdash?
Gemara's Source: The Tradition from Rabbi Yehoshua bar Mama Transmitting via Rabbi Yehoshua ben Chananya
The Chain of Tradition: Why Rabbi Eliezer Skips the Middle Link (Rabbi Yehoshua bar Mama)

macro_segments.title: Arbitrary Laws and the Limits of Logical Derivation: Rabbi Akiva's Kal Vachomer Rejected by Rabbi Eliezer and Validated by Rabbi Yehoshua

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Logical Argument: A Bone Shiur Kedarkalta Should Require Nezir Galui on Blood
Rabbi Eliezer's Dismissal: No Kal Vachomer in These Laws
Rabbi Yehoshua's Validation: Good Argument, But These Are Halacha L'Moshe Misinai
Conceptual Context: Rabbi Eliezer's Tradition-Based Approach vs. Rabbi Akiva's Rational Derivation

macro_segments.title: Halacha L'Moshe Misinai as an Exception to Kal Vachomer: The Bone Exception Cannot Serve as a Logical Premise

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Question: Which Rule Is the Halacha L'Moshe Misinai—the Bone or the Blood?
Beraita's Resolution: The Bone Shiur Is Halacha L'Moshe Misinai; Blood Revi'is Derives from Kal Vachomer
The Principle: Cannot Derive Kal Vachomer from a Halacha L'Moshe Misinai Exception

macro_segments.title: Two Nzirim with Uncertain Tumah Status: The Safek Tamei Scenario and Its Resolution Through Paired Offerings

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah Scenario: One of Two Nzirim Became Tamei, but the Witness Doesn't Know Which

macro_segments.title: Two Nzirim with Uncertain Tumah Status: The Safek Tamei Scenario and Its Resolution Through Paired Offerings (continued)

micro_segments.title: Halachic Framework: Safek Tumah B'Reshut Hayachid (Presumed Tamei) vs. B'Reshut Harabim (Presumed Tahor)
Gemara's Question: Is This Reshut Hayachid or Reshut Harabim Given the Third Witness?
Resolution: Reshut Hayachid Because the Witness Viewed from a Distance; Retaining Safek Status Rather Than Vadai
Practical Solution: Each Nezir Brings Paired Offerings (Tumah and Tahara) with Attribution Stipulation
The Two-Nezir Advantage: No Conditional Offerings Required; Each Brings Complete Sets Directed to the Appropriate Person

macro_segments.title: The Problem of Double Shaving: Why Permit a Nezir to Shave Twice When One May Be Tahor?

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's Difficulty: If One Nezir Was Tahor, Shaving Him a Second Time Violates Hakafat Rosh
Shmuel's Solution: This Case Involves a Woman or Minor Who Is Exempt from the Hakafah Prohibition
Inference from Shmuel's Answer: Proof That Hakafat Kol Harosh Violates the Hakafah Prohibition

macro_segments.title: The Shaver and Shavee as Independent Subjects: Rav Huna vs. the Prohibition of Cutting Sideburns

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Position: An Adult Male Shaver Is Obligated Regardless of the Shavee's Status
Rav Adda bar Ahava's Challenge: How Do You Allow Your Wife to Shave Your Children's Hair?
Huna's Response: A Woman Is Exempt; Therefore, a Woman Shaver Can Perform the Action
The Curse Episode: Rav Adda bar Ahava's Words That Rav Huna Had No Further Children
Summary of Rav Huna's Model: Adult Males Cannot Be Shaver or Shavee; Women and Minors Can Fill Either Role

topical_tags.term: Reshut Hayachid vs. Reshut Harabim (Private vs. Public Domain)
Shaver and Shavee (Cutter and Person Cut)
